scope.summary: Implement a `SolverMode::PreciseCollision` variant (or a `precise_self_collision: bool` flag in `SimRunParams`) that doubles the self-collision narrow-phase solve iterations per substep and tightens the repulsion alpha, mirroring MD's Precise Self-Collision mode for tight multi-layer garments
acceptance_criteria: `SimRunParams` gains `precise_self_collision: bool` (default `false`); when `true`, the self-collision narrow phase (COL-SELF-006) runs a second correction pass per substep with a tighter alpha value (e.g. half the standard `alpha_self_collision`)
The `SELF_INTERSECTION` ValidationDescriptor (MT-112) uses a stricter residual threshold when `precise_self_collision = true` (pair-count ceiling tightened by at least 50%)
An integration test drapes a two-layer jacket over a bodice and asserts `SELF_INTERSECTION` passes with `precise_self_collision = true` but fails or produces a higher pair-count with `false`
Precise mode does not break the determinism envelope contract (MT-038): same seed, same backend, same params -> same result
scope.risk_if_missed: Multi-layer garment stacking (coat over jacket over shirt) produces visible inter-layer penetrations that the standard self-collision pass cannot fully resolve; operators cannot match MD-quality tight-fit stacking for hero garments.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
